summary: The primary characteristic of fetal alcohol syndrome involves reduced head size, while macrocephaly does not align with this condition; therefore, it can be excluded from its sequela list. Key anatomical abnormalities such as a thin corpus callosum further confirm the distinctive profile associated with FAS.
